paper_title: Stability Analysis and Modeling of Phase Separation in Organic Molecules

methods.description: This method analyzes the stability of a spatially uniform system by examining whether small fluctuations in concentrations will grow or decay. It involves expanding the system equations to linear order and solving for the dispersion relation to determine conditions for spinodal decomposition.

steps.description: Start from a spatially uniform system with all concentrations at their equilibrium values and analyze small fluctuations.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze stability of equilibrium concentrations with small fluctuations

steps.description: Insert fluctuations into the equilibrium concentrations and expand up to linear order.
steps.searchable_summary: expand equilibrium concentrations with fluctuations to linear order

steps.description: Solve the linear system of homogeneous equations for the determinant of the coefficient matrix.
steps.searchable_summary: solve linear system for determinant of coefficient matrix

steps.description: Analyze the solutions of the cubic equation to determine the dispersion relation ω(q).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze cubic equation solutions for dispersion relation

steps.description: Plot examples of the dispersion relation for different dissociation constants.
steps.searchable_summary: plot dispersion relation for varying dissociation constants

methods.description: This method models the aggregation of organic molecules and their phase separation using the Cahn-Hilliard model. It involves setting up reaction-diffusion equations and solving them numerically to study the role of phosphate ions in the process.

steps.description: Set up the reaction equation A + B ⇋ C to model the aggregation process.
steps.searchable_summary: define reaction equation for aggregation process

steps.description: Use the Cahn-Hilliard model to describe the phase separation process.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Cahn-Hilliard model for phase separation

steps.description: Set up reaction-diffusion equations for the evolution of concentrations of components A, B, and C.
steps.searchable_summary: define reaction-diffusion equations for component concentrations

steps.description: Solve the system of nonlinear partial differential equations numerically using MATLAB and Comsol Multiphysics.
steps.searchable_summary: numerically solve PDEs using MATLAB and Comsol

steps.description: Analyze the conditions under which a uniform system becomes unstable and spinodal decomposition can occur.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze conditions for instability and spinodal decomposition
